11.name: Grilled Chicken with Fluffy Jasmine Rice
11.description: A classic family lunch. Chicken in smoked paprika marinade alongside jasmine rice that always turns out perfect.
11.intro: A classic family lunch. Chicken in smoked paprika marinade alongside jasmine rice that always turns out perfect. The marinade adds depth, and frying the rice in oil before cooking is the secret to separate, fluffy grains.

steps.title: Marinade
steps.text: Mix 1/4 cup olive oil, juice of half a lemon, lemon zest, 3 crushed garlic cloves, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp black pepper.

steps.title: Marinate chicken
steps.text: Coat 1 kg chicken pieces well with the marinade. Marinate for at least 30 minutes (up to overnight in the fridge).

steps.title: Rice base
steps.text: Heat 3 tbsp oil in a pot. Add 2 cups dry jasmine rice and fry for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the grains become translucent.

steps.title: Cook rice
steps.text: Add 3.5 cups boiling water and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring to a boil, cover and reduce to lowest heat for 18 minutes. Turn off heat and leave covered 5 more minutes.

steps.title: Grill chicken
steps.text: Heat a grill or grill pan to high. Grill chicken 10-15 minutes per side (depending on thickness) until deep golden char marks appear.

steps.title: Chimichurri (optional)
steps.text: Hand-chop (not food processor!) 1 bunch parsley, 1/2 bunch cilantro, 3 garlic cloves and 1/4 red onion. Mix with 1 tsp oregano, 1/2 tsp chili, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper, 3 tbsp red wine vinegar and 1/2 cup olive oil. Let rest 30 minutes before serving — the flavors need to merge.

11.proTip: Take the chicken out of the fridge 20 minutes before grilling — room temperature chicken grills more evenly. The chimichurri improves overnight in the fridge.

9.name: Kid-Friendly Spaghetti Bolognese
9.description: Rich Bolognese with root vegetable soffritto 'hidden' in the sauce — nutritious, delicious and picky-eater approved.
9.intro: A good Bolognese needs depth of flavor. For kids, the challenge is texture and acidity. The technique here is blending root vegetables (soffritto) to an almost invisible texture — natural sweetness that balances the tomato acidity.

steps.title: Soffritto
steps.text: Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a wide pot. Sauté grated onion, 2 grated carrots and diced celery on medium-low heat. The goal is to soften them into a sweet paste — this is the nutritional and flavor base.

steps.title: Brown the meat
steps.text: Turn up heat and add 500g ground beef. Break into the smallest pieces possible with a fork — big chunks are the #1 reason kids reject it. Brown until color changes.

steps.title: Build the sauce
steps.text: Add 2 heaping tbsp tomato paste and fry for 1 minute. Add crushed tomatoes, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp oregano and 1 tsp salt.

steps.title: Secret ingredient + simmer
steps.text: Add 1 tbsp date syrup (silan) — this is the secret that balances acidity. Add ½ cup water, stir well. Cover and simmer on low for at least 30 minutes.

steps.title: Cook & serve
steps.text: Cook spaghetti al dente. Check sauce consistency — add water if too thick, cook uncovered if too thin. Toss pasta with sauce and serve.

9.proTip: If kids are texture-sensitive — blend the onion and carrot in a blender with a little water before frying. They'll vanish into the sauce while adding tons of vitamins and sweetness.

10.name: Caramelized Rice Paper Malawach
10.description: Light and crispy malawach made from rice paper with egg batter and date syrup. Golden color, caramel aroma and delicate sweetness.
10.intro: Rice paper malawach is the lighter, crispier, faster version of the classic Yemeni dish. Using rice paper instead of oily dough makes it airy, while the date syrup in the batter gives it a deep golden color, amazing caramel aroma and delicate sweetness.

detailedIngredients.name: Grated tomatoes
detailedIngredients.note: Seasoned with salt and olive oil

steps.title: Make the batter
steps.text: Beat 1 egg well. Add 2 tbsp water/milk, 1 level tsp date syrup and 1/4 tsp cinnamon. Mix until smooth.

steps.title: Prepare rice paper
steps.text: Briefly dip a rice paper sheet in warm water (2-3 seconds only). Place on a clean surface.

steps.title: Layer and fold
steps.text: Brush batter on the sheet. Stack another dipped sheet on top. Fold into a square or triangle.

steps.title: Pan fry
steps.text: Heat a non-stick pan with a little olive oil on medium heat. Fry the malawach 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crispy.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Fold in half and plate. Arrange 1-2 halved hard boiled eggs alongside, a bowl of grated tomato (seasoned with salt and olive oil), drizzle olive oil and garnish with chili flakes, fresh basil and black sesame.

10.proTip: Use large rice papers (22cm) for crispier results. If you don't have date syrup, use a teaspoon of honey, but silan gives the perfect caramel color.

12.name: One-Pot Chicken & Orzo Bake
12.description: One-pan family dinner. Chicken thighs with crispy orzo in tomato sauce and warming spices.
12.intro: The ultimate comfort food. Chicken thighs roasted on a bed of orzo that absorbs all the juices and becomes irresistibly crispy on top. One pan, no fuss, feeds the whole family.

steps.title: Season chicken
steps.text: Season chicken with salt, paprika, hawaij and a drizzle of olive oil.

steps.title: Sear chicken
steps.text: Sear chicken in a hot pan skin-side down for 5 minutes until golden. Set aside.

steps.title: Build base
steps.text: In same pan, sauté onion and garlic until golden. Add orzo and toast with spices and oil.

steps.title: Add liquids
steps.text: Add boiling water, chicken broth powder and silan. Place chicken on top.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Cover and bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until orzo is cooked and chicken is done.

12.proTip: Coat orzo in oil and spices before adding liquid — this keeps each piece separate and flavorful.

13.name: Classic Shepherd's Pie
13.description: Rich seasoned meat filling topped with creamy mashed potatoes, baked until golden. Pure comfort food.
13.intro: The ultimate comfort dish. A rich, savory meat filling with onion, garlic and root vegetables, topped with a generous layer of creamy mashed potatoes and baked until golden on top. One of those dishes that makes the whole house smell amazing.

steps.title: Cook filling
steps.text: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil. Sauté diced red onion, 2 diced carrots and 5 minced garlic cloves until soft. Add 500g ground beef and brown well. Season with paprika, cumin and salt. Add ½ cup water and simmer 15 minutes.

steps.title: Make mash
steps.text: Boil 5 quartered potatoes until very soft (~20 min). Drain and mash with 2 tbsp butter, ¼ cup milk, 1 tbsp olive oil and ½ tsp salt until creamy and smooth.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.text: Pour the meat filling into a baking dish. Spread the mashed potatoes evenly on top. Score the surface with a fork for golden peaks.

steps.title: Bake
steps.text: Bake at 180°C for 25-30 minutes until the top is golden.

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Great with a simple green salad.

13.proTip: Add a sprig of fresh thyme to the meat filling while simmering — it adds an incredible aromatic depth that takes the dish to the next level.

14.name: Spelt Pita Bread with Garlic
14.description: Simple homemade spelt pitas with a garlic and parsley topping, pan-fried to perfection.
14.intro: Simple, satisfying homemade pitas made with spelt flour and yogurt. Pan-fried until puffy and golden, then topped with a generous garlic, olive oil and parsley spread on both sides.

steps.title: Make dough
steps.text: Mix yogurt with baking soda in a large bowl. Add whole wheat flour and salt. Knead just until the dough comes together (add flour if too sticky).

steps.title: Divide and flatten
steps.text: Divide into 4-5 equal balls. Roll each into a flat pita shape.

steps.title: Rest
steps.text: Let the pitas rest for 10 minutes (important for texture).

steps.title: Make topping
steps.text: Mix olive oil, 3-4 crushed garlic cloves and chopped parsley.

steps.title: Cook
steps.text: Heat a pan with a little oil or butter on heat level 10. Fry each pita for a few minutes, flipping every 30 seconds until puffed and golden on both sides.

steps.title: Finish
steps.text: Immediately spread the garlic-parsley mixture generously on both sides of each hot pita.

14.proTip: The key is a very hot pan and frequent flipping every 30 seconds. This lets the pita puff up and cook evenly without burning.

15.name: Golden Chicken & Root Vegetable Roast
15.description: A colorful one-pan family meal. Chicken, sweet potato, potato, zucchini, onion and roasted lemon with turmeric and paprika.
15.intro: Everything in one pan, straight to the oven. Chicken roasted with root vegetables in a golden turmeric-paprika-cumin seasoning. The roasted lemon at the end ties it all together with an incredible caramelized citrus flavor.

detailedIngredients.name: Lemon
detailedIngredients.note: Half for seasoning, 1 whole halved for roasting

steps.title: Make seasoning
steps.text: Mix 1/4 cup olive oil, juice of half a lemon, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, 1.5 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp black pepper.

steps.title: Prep chicken
steps.text: Place 1 kg chicken drumsticks in a large baking dish. Pour half the seasoning over the chicken and massage well.

steps.title: Add vegetables
steps.text: Add all vegetables: sweet potatoes, potatoes, 2 carrots in diagonal slices, zucchini, onion wedges, halved garlic head and halved lemon. Pour remaining seasoning and toss.

steps.title: Roast
steps.text: Preheat oven to 200C. Roast for 60 minutes until chicken is golden and vegetables are tender. Stir vegetables once halfway through.

steps.title: Finish
steps.text: Remove from oven. Squeeze the roasted lemon over everything and serve directly from the pan.

15.proTip: The roasted lemon is the secret! Roasting transforms it into something sweet and deep. Squeeze it generously over everything before serving.

16.name: Green Goddess Egg Salad on Sourdough
16.description: Rich egg salad with avocado, pickles and fresh herbs on toasted sourdough. A balancing dinner that nourishes blood and clears heat.
16.intro: A light, refreshing dinner combining rich eggs with creamy avocado, crunchy pickles and aromatic fresh herbs. The dill and mustard activate digestion, the arugula clears liver heat, and the pickles break down heaviness from previous meals.

detailedIngredients.name: Celery (optional)
detailedIngredients.note: Peeled and micro-diced. Drains fluids and clears stomach heat.

steps.title: Prep eggs
steps.text: Roughly mash 6 peeled hard boiled eggs with a fork (keep it chunky for texture).

steps.title: Make dressing
steps.text: Mix 1 tbsp mayo, 1 tsp mustard, 1 tbsp olive oil, juice of 1/4 lemon, black pepper and sea salt.

steps.title: Combine
steps.text: Add half a mashed avocado, 2 chopped pickled cucumbers, chopped dill and celery (if using) to the eggs. Gently fold in the dressing.

steps.title: Assemble & serve
steps.text: Toast the sourdough slices. Top each generously with egg salad. Scatter avocado cubes, arugula leaves and freshly cracked black pepper on top.

16.proTip: Celery is optional but makes a real difference! Peel the outer fibers and dice microscopically so you get the crunch and drainage benefit without it dominating the flavor.

17.name: Fermented Purple Cabbage with Lemon, Garlic & Honey
17.description: Homemade fermented cabbage rich in probiotics. Garlic and lemon boost immunity, honey balances. Ready in 10 days.
17.intro: A simple homemade fermentation process rich in natural probiotics. The combination of purple cabbage with garlic, lemon and honey creates a complex, surprising flavor — sour-sweet-spicy. Perfect as a topping on toast with egg, in salads, or alongside any main dish.

steps.title: Prep cabbage
steps.text: Slice 1 medium purple cabbage very thin. Transfer to a large bowl and sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Massage (critical!)
steps.text: Massage the cabbage firmly with your hands for 5-10 minutes. Goal: break down cell walls until the cabbage releases plenty of liquid. This liquid becomes the brine.

steps.title: Season
steps.text: Add lemon zest, fresh lemon juice, 2 garlic cloves and 1-2 tbsp honey. Mix well.

steps.title: Pack into jar
steps.text: Transfer to a clean glass jar. Press down firmly until liquid rises above the cabbage. Place a weight or whole cabbage leaf on top.

steps.title: Ferment
steps.text: Close the jar (preferably with airlock lid, or open once daily). Leave on counter in a shaded spot for about 10 days at room temperature.

steps.title: Store
steps.text: After 10 days the flavors have intensified and the cabbage is ready. Store in the fridge — keeps for months.

17.proTip: The cabbage must stay below the liquid line to prevent mold! If not enough liquid, add a bit of brine (1 tsp salt per cup water).

18.name: Classic Cheesecake Loaf with White Topping
18.description: Classic cheesecake in a loaf pan — crunchy petit beurre base, creamy cheese filling and a silky sour cream topping.
18.intro: An elegant cheesecake baked in a loaf pan — compact, beautiful and perfect for slicing. Crunchy cookie base, delicate vanilla cheese filling, and a white sour cream topping that gives it the perfect look. Slow baking at 160°C keeps it creamy without cracks.

detailedIngredients.name: Petit beurre cookies
detailedIngredients.note: Half a package, crushed to fine crumbs

steps.title: Make the base
steps.text: Crush 100g petit beurre cookies to fine crumbs and mix with 50g melted butter until it resembles wet sand. Press firmly into the bottom of a loaf pan and freeze for 10 minutes to set.

steps.title: Make the filling
steps.text: Preheat oven to 160°C. In a bowl, whisk by hand 375g cream cheese, 100ml sour cream and 1/2 cup sugar. Add 2 eggs one at a time, whisking just until absorbed. Fold in 2 tbsp vanilla pudding powder and 1 tbsp cornstarch until smooth.

steps.title: First bake
steps.text: Remove pan from freezer, pour the cheese filling over the cookie base and bake for 40-45 minutes. The cake is ready when the edges are set and the center still jiggles slightly (like jelly).

steps.title: White topping
steps.text: While the cake bakes, mix 200ml sour cream, 1.5 tbsp sugar and a little vanilla extract. When the first bake is done, remove the cake (don't turn off the oven!), spread the topping evenly and return to the oven for 10 more minutes.

steps.title: Cool and set
steps.text: Remove from oven and let cool completely at room temperature. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours (overnight is best) before slicing. Cooling is critical for the cake's structure.

18.proTip: Never over-mix! Excessive mixing incorporates air into the batter and creates cracks. A hand whisk is better than a mixer — cheesecake loves gentleness.
